title: Proof Claims Token
description: JSON Schema for PCT payload validation — Proof Claims Token Specification v0.1

pct_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this PCT instance. Must be a UUID v4.

issued_at.description: Timestamp at which the PCT was issued, in seconds since Unix epoch (UTC).

valid_from.description: Timestamp from which the PCT is valid. May equal issued_at.

expires_at.description: Timestamp after which the PCT is no longer valid.

subject_id.description: Identifier for the dataset, data flow, or processing subject.

data_origin.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the jurisdiction where the data was originally collected.

bases.description: The applicable lawful basis or bases.

framework.description: The regulatory framework under which the lawful basis is assessed.

allowed_purposes.description: The purposes for which the data may be used.

consent_status.description: Required when lawful_basis includes consent. True indicates valid, informed, current consent exists.

consent_record_ref.description: Reference to an external consent record.

permitted_regions.description: Country codes in which processing is permitted.

restricted_regions.description: Country codes in which processing is explicitly prohibited.

residency_required.description: If true, data must remain within permitted_regions at all times.

sovereignty_framework.description: Reference to a sovereignty or adequacy framework.

permitted_destinations.description: Country codes to which transfer is permitted.

transfer_mechanism.description: The legal mechanism authorising the transfer.

transfer_mechanism_ref.description: Reference to the specific instrument authorising the transfer.

onward_transfer_permitted.description: Whether the recipient may further transfer the data.

retention_limit.description: ISO 8601 duration. The maximum period for which the data may be retained (e.g. P2Y).

automated_decision_flag.description: Set to true if the data may be used in automated decision-making subject to Article 22 GDPR.

data_hash.description: Cryptographic hash of the canonical serialised form of the data payload at the time of token issuance. Base64url-encoded.

hash_algorithm.description: The hashing algorithm used to produce data_hash. MD5 and SHA-1 are prohibited.

hash_scope.description: Defines what was hashed. full_payload for entire payload, merkle_root for Merkle tree structure.

data_format.description: MIME type or format descriptor of the data payload at the time of hashing (e.g. application/json, text/csv).

model_id.description: Identifier for the AI model being invoked.

model_region.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 code of the jurisdiction in which the model will process the data.

risk_tier.description: AI risk classification under the EU AI Act or equivalent.

prohibited_use_check.description: Attests that the intended use has been checked against the list of prohibited AI applications.

human_oversight_required.description: Whether human review of the AI output is required before any decision is actioned.

training_data_flag.description: Set to true if the data may be used to train, fine-tune, or evaluate the model.

output_retention_permitted.description: Whether AI-generated outputs derived from this data may be retained.

conformity_assessment_ref.description: For high-risk AI systems, reference to the conformity assessment documentation.

extensions.description: Extension namespace claims using the x-{framework}:{field} prefix convention.
